question: [File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with an empty or zero-length runningHash byte array, causing subscribers that rely on the running hash for integrity verification to accept a message with no hash proof, breaking the chain of trust for all subsequent messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a chunkNum greater than chunkTotal, causing subscribers that reassemble chunked messages to enter an invalid state, potentially processing incomplete or corrupted multi-chunk transactions?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with chunkNum=1 and chunkTotal=1 for a message that is actually part of a multi-chunk sequence, causing poll() to return it as a complete standalone message and causing subscribers to process an incomplete transaction as if it were whole?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes the Flux.defer(() -> poll(context)) pipeline to emit an error signal, which combined with Retry.backoff(Long.MAX_VALUE, ...) causes an infinite retry storm that starves the boundedElastic scheduler and denies service to all other gRPC subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a message payload containing specially crafted bytes that, when deserialized by downstream consumers of the Flux<TopicMessage>, trigger a deserialization vulnerability and achieve remote code execution?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with an extremely large message byte array, causing poll() to load it into memory via Flux.fromStream(), exhausting heap memory and causing an OutOfMemoryError that crashes the gRPC service for all subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a consensusTimestamp just 1 nanosecond after the filter's endTime, causing the boundary condition in findByFilter() (cb.lessThan(consensusTimestamp, endTime)) to exclude it, but if the tampered message is at endTime exactly, it may be included or excluded inconsistently, causing non-deterministic message delivery?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: isComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes pageSize.get() to equal retrieverProperties.getMaxPageSize() on the first poll in throttled mode, triggering isComplete() to return true immediately and preventing any further messages from being delivered to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes the newFilter built via filter.toBuilder().limit(pageSize).startTime(startTime).build() to have a startTime in the past, causing poll() to re-fetch already-delivered messages and deliver duplicates to the subscriber?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a consensusTimestamp equal to filter.getStartTime(), causing it to be included in the very first poll() result and displacing or reordering legitimate messages that share the same start boundary?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes the doOnCancel(context::onComplete) callback to be triggered unexpectedly, logging misleading completion statistics and masking the fact that the stream was cancelled due to tampered data?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: onComplete()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes onComplete() to log a misleadingly high message rate (rate = 1000.0 * total.get() / elapsed), obscuring the fact that tampered messages inflated the count and making operators believe the system is healthy when it is not?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a topicId set to a wildcard or null-equivalent EntityId, causing findByFilter() to return messages from all topics if the equality predicate (cb.equal(root.get(TOPIC_ID), filter.getTopicId())) fails to handle null correctly, leaking all topic messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a sequenceNumber that is lower than the previous message's sequenceNumber, causing subscribers that rely on monotonically increasing sequence numbers to detect a replay attack to miss the detection and process a replayed transaction?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a sequenceNumber identical to a previously delivered message, causing subscribers to process a duplicate transaction as if it were new, leading to double-spend or double-execution vulnerabilities in downstream systems?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes the repeatWhen condition (!context.isComplete()) to never become true, causing the polling loop to repeat indefinitely (numRepeats = Long.MAX_VALUE in throttled mode) and holding a scheduler thread forever, starving other subscribers?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a message field containing a null byte sequence that, when returned by poll() and processed by the gRPC serializer, causes a serialization error that terminates the subscriber's stream?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a payerAccountId encoding that maps to a different valid EntityId when decoded by EntityIdConverter, causing the subscriber to see a different payer than the one who actually submitted the transaction?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a consensusTimestamp that is identical to the filter's startTime minus 1, causing the greaterThanOrEqualTo predicate in findByFilter() to exclude it, but if the attacker then submits a second message at startTime, the first tampered message poisons the running hash chain silently?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a runningHash that is a valid hash of a different message content, causing subscribers to verify the hash successfully but receive incorrect message data, achieving a hash collision-based content substitution attack?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: PollingContext()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes the PollingContext constructor to select the unthrottled path (throttled=false), where numRepeats = unthrottled.getMaxPolls() is finite, causing the retriever to stop polling after maxPolls iterations even if legitimate messages are still pending?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a chunkTotal of Integer.MAX_VALUE, causing subscribers that allocate buffers based on chunkTotal to exhaust memory when attempting to reassemble the chunked message?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a negative chunkNum, causing subscribers that use chunkNum as an array index to throw an ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException or access incorrect buffer positions when reassembling chunked messages?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: retrieve()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction that causes the Micrometer observation tap in retrieve() to record incorrect metrics (e.g., inflated message counts or latencies), causing operators to make incorrect scaling or alerting decisions based on falsified telemetry?

[File: 'File Name: grpc/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/grpc/retriever/PollingTopicMessageRetriever.java -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: poll()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a tampered transaction with a consensusTimestamp that is exactly equal to Long.MIN_VALUE, causing the startTime computation (last.getConsensusTimestamp() + 1 = Long.MIN_VALUE + 1) to produce a very small negative number, causing findByFilter() to return all messages from the beginning of the database?
